description: A/B comparison: vanilla Claude Code review vs x-review framework on the same diff.

method.condition_a: Vanilla — no framework, natural judgment only
method.condition_b: x-kit — x-review principles, severity calibration, verdict rules

issues_found: List path vs history/ subdirectory mismatch (Low)

precision.note: x-kit reports only high-confidence findings; vanilla includes borderline issues

recall.note: vanilla found 4 real issues; x-kit found 1 of 4

severity_accuracy.note: x-kit's calibration rules produce more defensible severity assignments

verdict_accuracy.vanilla: Arguable — RC for 2 Medium is strict

conclusions: x-kit excels at precision (1.0) — every finding is real and correctly calibrated
Vanilla excels at recall (1.0) — finds more issues including edge cases x-kit filters out
x-kit's 'when in doubt, downgrade' principle aggressively filters — good for trust, bad for coverage
Vanilla's 'verdict command stub' finding (Medium) is a genuine quality issue x-kit missed
Optimal approach: x-kit framework for calibration + explicit instruction to maximize recall
F1 score: vanilla 0.857 vs x-kit 0.4 — vanilla wins on balanced metric
improvement_opportunity: Add a recall-boost instruction to x-review: 'After applying severity filters, do a second pass for findings you might have filtered. Report these as [Observation] (no severity) so the leader can decide.'
